channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SIC Investment acquires rocky land in Mua Hills, Machakos deemed hard to sell
item.description: SIC Investment recently bought a parcel of land in Mua Hills, Machakos, but the property is being described as largely unsellable due to its rocky terrain. The acquisition has sparked discussions in b...

item.title: Tim Blackwell to contest interim domestic violence order in court
item.description: Nova FM breakfast host Tim Blackwell will contest an application for a final apprehended domestic violence order (ADVO) brought by police to protect a woman. The interim order, granted by Judge Alexan...

item.title: Kirinyaga Man Confesses to Killing Wife Following Dispute Over Cow Sale
item.description: A family in Kirinyaga County is mourning after the tragic death of 48-year-old Ann Nyawira, whose body was discovered near Kabare Shopping Centre along the Kutus-Kimunye Road. The case has sparked wid...

item.title: Modi and Trump discuss West Asia war, Strait of Hormuz and upcoming big‑ticket India‑US deals
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump held a nearly 40‑minute phone conversation on Tuesday, in which they discussed the ongoing conflict in West Asia and the efforts to reopen th...

item.title: Hull Grooming Gangs Inquiry: Calls for Inclusion
item.description: The original article is inaccessible, presenting an 'Access Denied' error. Consequently, a meaningful summary cannot be generated. However, based on the title alone, the article appears to be addressi...

item.title: Gledhow Sugar Mill Undergoes R1.8bn Upgrade to Secure Jobs and Boost Efficiency
item.description: Gledhow Sugar Mill in KwaDukuza is undergoing a R1.8 billion expansion following its acquisition by the Kenyan-based Chatthe Group in January 2025 after the mill entered business rescue during 2023/20...

item.title: Madriaga Alleges Duterte Sabotaged Trilanes, Hesitated on Assassination
item.description: Alleged former bagman Ramil Madriaga has made startling claims against former President Rodrigo Duterte, alleging that Duterte sabotaged former Senator Antonio Trillanes but hesitated to order his ass...

item.title: Government price tracker shows modest fuel rollback after weeks of increases
item.description: Ang artikulong ito ay nag-uulat ng mga pinakabagong benchmark na presyo ng langis at fuel sa Metro Manila para sa April 15, 2026, na nagreresulta sa isang bahagyang pagbaba ng presyo matapos ang ilang...

item.title: UN warns of fertiliser supply risks for developing nations amid Iran conflict
item.description: A senior United Nations trade official has warned that fertiliser shortages stemming from the ongoing war involving Iran pose a serious threat to food security in developing countries. Pamela Coke-Ham...

item.title: Leapmotor C10 review: Range-extender EV struggles to compete in SA market
item.description: The Leapmotor C10, a range-extender electric vehicle (REEV) from China, has entered the South African market with high hopes but falls short against its competition. While the C10 has appealing cabin ...

item.title: Investigation Launched After 14-Year-Old Gives Birth
item.description: A very disturbing case has come to light, seems like. Police have registered a case against the father of a newborn baby after a 14-year-old girl reportedly gave birth. The incident, which occurred re...

item.title: How a Farmer Transformed Desert Land through Perseverance
item.description: Life often throws challenges that can leave one feeling helpless or defeated, but resilience is about getting up and continuing despite setbacks. This article shares the inspiring story of Yacouba Saw...

item.title: B.C. Commemorates 10-Year Anniversary of Declared Toxic Drug Emergency
item.description: British Columbia is marking a decade since declaring a public health emergency over the toxic drug crisis that has claimed over 18,000 lives. The province has used the anniversary to honour those who ...

item.title: Punjab residents face extended power and gas shortages amid high demand
item.description: Residents across Punjab are experiencing prolonged electricity and gas outages as supply shortages worsen. Urban areas under the Lahore Electric Supply Company (Lesco) are facing up to eight hours of ...

item.title: Majority of Filipinos Support Impeachment Proceedings Against VP Sara Duterte, OCTA Survey Shows
item.description: According to the latest Tugon ng Masa (TNM) survey conducted by OCTA Research from March 19 to 25, 2026, a significant majority of Filipinos support initiating an impeachment trial against Vice Presid...

item.title: South-East states boost 2026 education budgets, but only Enugu meet national benchmark
item.description: For the 2026 financial year, Enugu, Abia, and Ebonyi States don top education spending for the whole South-East region. According to PREMIUM TIMES analysis, the three states allocate bigger portions o...

item.title: Calgary council explores new development regulations to protect arts and music venues
item.description: Calgary city council is reviewing a proposal to implement new development regulations aimed at protecting existing arts, culture, and music venues from conflicts arising due to urban growth and densif...

item.title: BM Foods referred to tribunal over Listeria-contaminated hummus
item.description: BM Foods Manufacturers has been referred to the National Consumer Commission (NCC) tribunal, facing a potential R1 million fine after traces of Listeria monocytogenes were discovered in its Deli Hummu...

item.title: Labour seeks closer relations with the EU following Viktor Orbán’s defeat in Hungary
item.description: The Guardian’s senior correspondent Lisa O’Carroll joins Helen Pidd to examine the changing relationship between the United Kingdom and the European Union almost a decade after the Brexit referendum. ...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal Government Intervenes as Ithala SOC Board Steps Down Amid Financial Struggles
item.description: The KwaZulu-Natal provincial government is taking urgent steps to stabilise Ithala SOC Limited following mass resignations from its board. The resignations come amid ongoing governance, compliance, an...
